page: Amazon Web Services c6g.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the custom AWS Graviton processor. Built on the 64-bit ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture, this server features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs operating at 2.5 GHz. It lacks hyperthreading, meaning each vCPU maps directly to a physical core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache per core, and 32 MB of shared L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type, and network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the c6g.4xlarge in the average tier for most multi-core and compute-heavy tasks. The stress-ng multi-core CPU benchmark registers at 24286.42, while the Geekbench multi-core score reaches 8581.0. Software compilation via Geekbench clang scores 15859.0, and ray tracing scores 13820.0, both indicating solid mid-range performance. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, with a Geekbench single-core score of 969.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1519.69. Cryptographic performance is also a weak point, as shown by a Passmark CPU encryption score of 2187.72, despite hardware-level AES support. Memory bandwidth is strong for cached reads at 946.86 GB/sec, but drops to 169.7 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Large block write bandwidth is measured at 71.54 GB/sec.

The c6g.4xlarge offers a highly cost-efficient profile for workloads that scale horizontally across dedicated ARM cores without requiring x86-specific instruction sets like AVX or AMX. It is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, static web serving (achieving 18.73 GB/sec throughput), and database operations, as evidenced by a Passmark database score of 10398.82 and 19.74 million Redis SET operations per second. It is less suitable for single-threaded tasks, heavy cryptographic processing, or large-scale LLM inference, where text generation speeds for a 7B model average a modest 10.53 tokens/sec.
description: Amazon Web Services c6g.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated physical ARM64 cores powered by the 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. It is configured with 32.0 GB of memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage or a GPU, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 24286.42 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8581.0, though single-core and cryptographic performance are relatively weak. The server is highly efficient for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and database operations like Redis, which registers 19.74 million SET operations per second. It is less suited for workloads requiring x86 vector extensions or high-performance single-threaded execution.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6g.4xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 server with 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering solid multi-core performance for web serving and databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6g.4xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server with 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps baseline network speed.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring dedicated physical cores and balanced multi-threaded performance for web serving and database workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 at 2.5 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Strong multi-threaded performance for software compilation and ray tracing
Efficient static web serving and Redis database operations
Lacks x86 instruction sets such as AVX and AMX
